reasoning: The Texas House has significantly altered the primary vehicle for a THC ban (SB 3) as of May 1, changing it from a total ban to a regulatory framework that allows edibles and drinks. Given the size of the Texas hemp industry ($8B+), the resulting loss in state revenue, and strong internal House opposition to a full ban (with HB 28 pushing for regulation instead), passage of a total ban before the June 2 Sine Die is unlikely. While Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick is pushing hard and threatening special sessions, Gov. Abbott has not explicitly made a total ban a priority, reducing the likelihood that the House will be forced to capitulate. Even if a special session is called, passing and signing a total ban by the July 1, 2025 deadline faces tight timing and identical legislative hurdles.
